lines.content: Hey, good morning Tim, this is Kevin with Einstein Moving Company. How you doing? I'm doing well. Thanks for asking. I just wanted to follow up with you on your trial day you know, apologize for taking a couple of days to get back to you. But I know you all, you all went out there or you went out with the guys on Monday. I just wanna see what you thought and you know, just follow up with on that.
lines.name: Khevan Dueck

lines.content: Oh, yeah, nor said it could take a couple of days you to get back. So, I figured it was coming.
lines.name: Green Richard - APP

lines.content: But yeah, it was a good job. My big thing was some, I probably should have told you during the interview process when I was in Korea I became a what's known as like a heat case heat exhausting. So one of them doing anything like labor intensive. I usually like, I don't necessarily take a lot of breaks with but it's like, one of our feel like myself is like overheating or anything like that. I kind of, you know slow down for a little bit and I don't know if that's gonna be an issue for you on your end in terms of you know job efficiency and stuff like that. So, definitely wanted to you know discuss that with you.
lines.name: Green Richard - APP

lines.content: It, I mean, it absolutely could be something that like you said it could you know, take a little bit of efficiency. Everyone moves it their own pace. So it's not something that it's definitely not something we can pick before it happens. It's more of a, you know, we do require a physical and a, and a drug test taken. So, if you are physically cleared by the medical professionals then there is, you know, we're not the medical professionals, we're not the ones to make that call. And then of course it's your comfort level. So, if it, if you feel like it's something that you can do your capable of and it's not something that you are, you know, for example, if you had a, you know, bad back or something you definitely wouldn't want to be moving, if someone comes in knowing that probably not a good idea to accept a job as a mover.
lines.name: Khevan Dueck

lines.content: So if it's something you feel comfortable with and it's something the medical professionals don't see an issue with then really, it's just about, you know, how it's managed day-to-day and you know, staying hydrated, doing what you gotta do, communicating that, communicating those, I guess situations to the team and the customer and of course, just, you know, making sure that we can stay efficient even if it means, you know, running things inside the home or taken on a little bit more of the, you know, the other aspects and communicating that with your team.
lines.name: Khevan Dueck

lines.content: Yeah, I would, I don't know how much they told you in terms of how I was on Monday, but I was definitely, like, communicating well with Noha and Daniel Notter in terms of me, he being I, is, there were a moment where like, I needed to take a break, cause either my arms are like, just shot, cause it's not, no, I don't hit the gym as much. So trying to get back in the Rym constantly living lifting things but then also like times of me kind of feeling like I was overheating, so I was like, I was taking a break, I had a lot of water whenever like, I finished the entire bottle whenever we were loading it up and then during our little break or lunch break when we went into the.
lines.name: Green Richard - APP

lines.content: The gas station, I got a, a big bottle like smart water and like, I pretty much almost finished that during the unload. So it is something I can manage, but then I was also like communicating in terms of like what I was doing. So, then they knew that it's like I was taken care of or what I was thinking of and not trying to like guess around it like their time. I was like I'm gonna go and try to collect all the stuff there still needs to be wrapped and then just start wrapping those or I'm gonna start moving visit at this time or something like that. So communication is definitely a big thing making sure we're all on the same page and all that stuff.
lines.name: Green Richard - APP

lines.content: Yeah, of course, well with all that you know, the feedback that I was given was, you know, you, you're, where you handled the day. Well, took direction great. Of course they did mentioned, you know, everyone needs a little bit of strength conditioning. Once they, you know, most people don't come in completely condition for the job. And, you know, everyone has a little bit of strength to, to build. But that's really the most critical thing that they had to mentioned. So with that, I don't see any reason why we wouldn't be, you know, if you're still interested we would like to offer that position to you.
lines.name: Khevan Dueck

lines.content: Yeah, I'll definitely take it, just trying to, there was a question I had that I couldn't ask you on Monday cause you're already gone but he was in terms of getting the payment for that day. Is that just gonna get rolled into the first pay period or like how does the pay periods actually work?
lines.name: Green Richard - APP

lines.content: Yeah, so it's always going to be paid by a paper check so it doesn't get rolled onto your first paycheck any, on like your direct deposit that paper check. I can have it written and ready for you whenever and then as far as the pay periods go we get paid biweekly. So tomorrow is, is a pay day, so that would have been from the 23rd of May through the 12th of June. The next pay day will be July 3rd. That'd be from the thirteenth to the twenty sixth and then, so on.
lines.name: Khevan Dueck

lines.content: Alright, I'm just trying to get.

lines.content: Caught up in terms of, you know, money. It probably until like the first paycheck I'll probably be doing like necessarily like, full-time double duty but a little bit like doors on the side just to keep some money coming in until our first my first paycheck like, it won't take me on the job or anything like that. lines.content: It's not that I necessarily like, try to, like, I go out of my way to do it. It just, something that happens is I usually become one of the more of a liable workers that you end up having. So, even if I'm doing my DoorDash it's on, on the side whenever like, for like a few hours whatever it's not gonna affect my, my performance on your job.

lines.content: Mm-hmm so just going forward. What can I expect in terms of like when to start and orientation and all that stuff?

lines.content: Our HR team will send over an offer letter later today and then from there, once you sign that it'll trigger a workflow to get your background check completed physical and your analysis all of those are gonna be once you sign the offer letter, it'll prompt you to fill in some information that, that'll give them the information to get those things started. The offer letter, I believe expires in.
lines.name: Khevan Dueck

lines.content: 48 hours and then once you fit, once you sign the offer letter than the background check, physical drug test, all that E passport stuff expires in 72 hours from the signing of the offer letter. So if you needed extra time for whatever reason, just let me know I can delay the offer letter but otherwise I was going to have the team send it, you know, as soon as they as they can this morning, this morning slash early afternoon.
